chapter_context.summary: Indicts the rebellious and polluted city of Jerusalem, rebuking her corrupt princes, evening wolves of judges, light prophets, and treacherous priests, but transitions to the purging of proud boasters, leaving an afflicted, humble, and truthful remnant, culminating in an ecstatic psalm of joy celebrating God as a victorious warrior who sings over His people with joy.

outline.text: Woe to the Rebellious City: Jerusalem's Corruption of Princes, Evening Wolves of Judges, Prophets, and Priests
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Woe to the Rebellious City: Jerusalem's Corruption of Princes, Evening Wolves of Judges, Prophets, and Priests (3:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Zephaniah.

outline.text: The Failure of Warning: Nations Cut Off as Examples, Yet Jerusalem Eagerly Corrupting All Her Doings
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Failure of Warning: Nations Cut Off as Examples, Yet Jerusalem Eagerly Corrupting All Her Doings (3:6-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Zephaniah.

outline.text: The Purged Remnant: Restoring a Pure Language and Leaving a Meek, Afflicted People Trusting in Yahweh's Name
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Purged Remnant: Restoring a Pure Language and Leaving a Meek, Afflicted People Trusting in Yahweh's Name (3:9-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Zephaniah.

outline.text: The Song of the Victorious King: Exhorting Daughter of Zion to Sing, God Relieving Their Affliction, and Singing Over Them with Joy
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Song of the Victorious King: Exhorting Daughter of Zion to Sing, God Relieving Their Affliction, and Singing Over Them with Joy (3:14-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Zephaniah.

key_themes.title: The Terrifying Day of the Lord
key_themes.description: Zephaniah provides one of the most intense descriptions of the Day of the Lord in the Old Testament, representing absolute stripping of creation and purification of human society.

key_themes.title: The Poor and Afflicted Remnant
key_themes.description: God reveals that the solution to societal decay is not royal might or wealth, but a humble, transparent remnant that relies entirely on His character.

devotional.question: Reflecting on Chapter 3, what details of the description of the Day of the Lord cause you to seek spiritual renewal and transparent devotion?
How does the final image of God as a triumphant warrior singing over you with quietness and joy reframe your understanding of His love?

cultural_historical.title: The Reign of Josiah and the Search for Pure Religion
cultural_historical.text: Zephaniah's ministry took place during the reign of Josiah (640–609 BC), likely before his radical temple reforms of 622 BC. The widespread syncretism and religious complacency described in Chapter 1 provide the backdrop for the urgent need of national purification.
